search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG239 B12 deficiency symptoms risk factors testing results follow-up 2024
site:dha.gov.ae vitamin B12 deficiency guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic vitamin B12 deficiency or autoimmune-gastritis diagnosis
automatic oral or intramuscular replacement, route, dose or interval
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Symptoms and one result do not independently establish diagnosis or cause.
All replacement choices, routes, doses and schedules were excluded.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People aged 16 years and older; paediatric cases and condition-specific pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, nitrous-oxide and neurological contexts require qualified review.
setting_applicability: Primary or endocrine follow-up of suspected or confirmed B12 deficiency.
UAE_applicability: Clinical domains transfer, but UAE laboratory ranges, medicine availability, prescribing, gastroenterology and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: NICE NG239 published 2024-03-06 was reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact recognition section supports onset, interval change, functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient concerns for a B12-deficiency review without confirming deficiency or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact test and result-context section supports focused assessment and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation, including supplement and assay context, without autonomously interpreting the result.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up section supports recording worsening or new symptoms, clinician concern, a clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net and arranged follow-up; medicine route and dose remain unsupported.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Cause-identification recommendations were reviewed, but no cause is inferred or inserted.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented autoimmune-gastritis, coeliac, medicine, diet, surgery and other cause-review context without asserting a cause.

unresolved_source_gaps: Specific symptoms, diet, surgery, medicines, nitrous oxide, blood count, B12 assay, MMA, homocysteine, antibodies, cause, diagnosis, treatment, dose and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
